text.title: Prompt Engineering — Evaluation and Debugging — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-engineering-evaluation methodology. Sources: Zheng et al. — MT-Bench / LLM-as-judge (arXiv:2306.05685); Anthropic — evaluating prompts guide; OpenAI Evals framework.

rule.statement: Every production prompt MUST have: a defined accuracy or quality baseline, a minimum acceptable threshold, and a regression test set of at least 10 representative inputs. Consistency must be measured across at least 3 independent runs on the same inputs.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline, teams cannot distinguish improvements from regressions. Without a threshold, prompts are deployed on subjective judgment. Without regression tests, every change risks breaking existing functionality.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `report` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r2).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `report` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r3).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `report` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r4).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `report` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
